query: A partner hospital sent us a 50,000-row patient CSV. Columns: patient_id, age, sex, diagnosis (free-text-looking), email, dob, outcome (binary). Generate a data dictionary I can hand to my team before we start modeling.
expected_behavior: Produces per-column entries that include at least: type, null %, unique count, and semantic class (e.g., identifier / continuous / categorical / text / boolean / datetime)

Correctly classifies high-cardinality free-text columns (e.g., diagnosis) as text rather than categorical, and correctly classifies unique-equal-row-count columns (e.g., patient_id) as identifier with a note not to use as a feature
Flags PII-shaped columns (e.g., email, dob) so the user is aware they require redaction or special handling before sharing or modeling
